title: CEO Commentary on Shares & Deposits: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Portfolio Composition
The credit union’s total member shares stood at $845 million as of March 31, 2026, reflecting a modest quarterly increase of 1.2%. This growth was driven primarily by gains in share drafts and regular shares, which together accounted for the bulk of the expansion.

Share Drafts represented the largest funding component at $254 million (29.9% of total), followed closely by Share Certificates at $253 million (30.1%). Regular Shares comprised 24.7%, while Money Market Shares made up 12.4%. IRA/Keogh Accounts contributed a smaller but meaningful portion at 2.8%, and All Other Shares rounded out the portfolio at 0.1%.

Share Drafts grew by 6.0% quarter over quarter, Share Certificates rose 1.5%, Regular Shares increased 7.9%, Money Market Shares declined slightly by 1.7%, IRA/Keogh Accounts remained flat (-0.0%), and Nonmember Deposits saw a negligible rise of 0.5%. The combination of growth in share drafts, certificates, and regular shares—offset partially by the slight decline in money market shares—means the funding mix is moving toward a more stable profile anchored by fixed-term instruments while retaining flexibility through continued reliance on demand-based products.

The credit union’s diversified deposit base continues to support its liquidity needs without overreliance on any single category. Management remains focused on preserving member relationships and managing cost of funds in an environment where balance sheet stability is increasingly important.
